steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify the right triangle setup
steps.content: The right angle is at P (shown by the square). The 30° angle is at R, making the angle at Q equal to 60°. The hypotenuse QR = 8. We need PQ, which is the side opposite the 30° angle at R.

steps.title: Apply the sine ratio
steps.content:
In a right triangle, $\sin(30^{\circ}) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{PQ}{QR}$. Since $\sin(30^{\circ}) = \frac{1}{2}$ and $QR = 8$:
$$\frac{PQ}{8} = \frac{1}{2}$$
$$PQ = 8 \cdot \frac{1}{2} = 4$$
The length of $\overline{PQ}$ is 4.
